rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the memcpy in the bad function that uses sizeof(structCharVoid) and explained it overwrites past charFirst into voidSecond, causing the overflow. They described the concrete mechanism and corruption of the pointer.
rationale.local_fix: They explicitly changed the length argument to sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst). This copies only the buffer capacity and avoids overwriting adjacent fields.
rationale.global_fix: Their global suggestion is vague ("bounded string type"/wrapper or strncpy) and not an explicit representation tying copy length to the destination's capacity. No concrete span/bounded-buffer design or code change was provided to enforce this invariant.
